title: Toilet cleaning fizz tabs
subtitle: Bicarbonate and citric acid tabs with tea tree for the toilet bowl
excerpt: Fizzing toilet cleaning tablets made from bicarbonate of soda and citric acid with tea tree essential oil. Drop one tab into the toilet bowl; the citric acid fizz loosens limescale and staining while the tea tree provides antimicrobial action. Press into a round mould and allow 24 hours to harden before use.
sourceNotes: Citric acid descaling chemistry; UK Health Security Agency, Guidance on household cleaning products for infection prevention and control (2023). Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014), tea tree antimicrobial properties.

content.text: A fizzing toilet cleaning tab works just like a bath bomb. Bicarbonate of soda is the base, citric acid is the acid. They fizz in the toilet water and let off carbon dioxide gas, which loosens limescale, staining, and grime on the bowl. Tea tree essential oil kills surface bacteria at the dose that ends up in the water. The fizzing carries the cleaner under the rim and into the water trap, where a brush often can't reach.

Humidity is the enemy of this recipe, exactly as with bath bombs. Work on a dry day, press quickly, and store the finished tabs sealed. Do not leave the mixture open in a humid bathroom. Tea tree essential oil is toxic to cats and dogs; if pets drink from the toilet bowl, omit the tea tree and substitute eucalyptus essential oil at the same weight.

items.name: Bicarbonate of soda
items.prepNote: fine bicarbonate of soda. 67% of the dry base. Sift to remove lumps before mixing.

items.name: Citric acid
items.prepNote: fine citric acid powder (food-grade). 33% of the dry base. The fizzing and descaling acid.

items.name: Tea tree essential oil
items.prepNote: Melaleuca alternifolia tea tree essential oil. 1.7% of the batch. The antimicrobial active. Toxic to cats and dogs.

items.name: Lemon essential oil
items.prepNote: Citrus limon lemon essential oil. Optional supplementary freshening. Omit for a plain tea tree version.

content.text: Sift the bicarbonate of soda into a large dry bowl. Sift the citric acid separately, then stir into the bicarbonate with a silicone spatula. Do not use a metal whisk; friction can cause localised fizzing. The dry mix should look uniform and pale.

Combine the essential oils in a small cup. Add to the dry base a few drops at a time, stirring constantly after each addition. Do not pour all the oil into one spot; the trace moisture in the oil triggers the acid-base reaction if it contacts a concentrated pile of both powders at once. Add slowly and stir immediately. The mixture should look like slightly damp sand and clump when pressed between the fingers.

Work quickly. Pack the mixture firmly into ice cube tray cavities or small round silicone moulds, pressing hard with a thumb to compact. Leave undisturbed for 24 hours. After setting, the tabs will be firm and retain their shape. Pop out of the mould and store in a sealed glass jar.

Drop one tab into the toilet bowl. It will fizz actively for 30-60 seconds. Leave for 10 minutes, then scrub with a toilet brush and flush. For stubborn limescale staining at the waterline, use two tabs and leave for 30 minutes before scrubbing.

6 months in a sealed, dry container. A bathroom cupboard is often too humid for long-term storage; a kitchen cupboard is better. Individually wrap each tab in cling film if you want to extend shelf life or keep them in a bathroom drawer without risk of early activation.
